title: FloodLAMP Inactivation Solution Prep v1.0
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"12n00","text":"The Inactivation Solution includes TCEP, EDTA, NaOH, and water. It should be made in a chemical fume hood, as the TCEP is caustic. It is very straightforward to make if starting with liquid-form TCEP. We have not made it with powder-form TCEP, which is about 30% cheaper at low volume retail prices from Sigma. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d7bj8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"n9fu","text":"We have yet to do long-term stability studies; however, we think it's fine stored at room temperature for at least a few weeks. If yellow Binding Solution does not go clear upon addition to an inactivated sample or PBS+Inactivation Soln, suspect that the Inactivation Solution may have degraded, and repeat with another aliquot or freshly-made Inactivation Solution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7r8al","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"19nop","text":"It has also been suggested by Albert Yu of Brandeis has recommended using a 1.4N NaOH concentration for saliva instead of 1.15N NaOH. NaOH should be scale up by 1.4/1.15 and water down for this. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8f04v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"85n73","text":"Individuals are responsible for the chemical safety training to safely complete this protocol. This procedure should be completed in a chem fume hood with appropriate PPE. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"8ejbd","text":"Both TCEP and EDTA should be handled cautiously as they can cause severe eye damage and are toxic if inhaled. See SDS for TCEP, EDTA, and NaOH for more safety information.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1tquk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Add all TCEP to tube, break glass vial tops off, using the 1000uL pipette extract the liquid with the vial inverted
Get amounts of TCEP, EDTA, NaOH and Ultrapure at volumes shown in chart below:
Vortex for 10s, be sure the tube is fully closed
Vortex for 10s, be sure the tube is fully closed
Vortex for 10s, be sure the tube is fully closed
creator: Randy True
